studyName: A Genomic Approach to Warfarin Dose Prescription in Admixed Caribbean Hispanics

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject IDs, sex, age at the recruitment, height, weight, warfarin daily and weekly doses, if warfarin was indicated to manage deep vein thrombosis, pulmonary embolism, atrial fibrillation, mechanical heart valve replacement, and stroke, if the patient has diabetes, congestive heart failure, and other conditions, if the patient uses aspirin, statins, and medications containing azoles, if the patient smokes, and African, Caucasian and Native (Zapotecas) parental proportions.

variables.name: AF
variables.description: If warfarin was indicated to manage atrial fibrillation

variables.name: African
variables.description: African parental proportion

variables.name: Age_recruitment
variables.description: Subject age at the moment of giving informed consent

variables.name: Aspirin
variables.description: If the patient uses aspirin

variables.name: Azoles
variables.description: If the patient uses medications containing azoles

variables.name: CHF
variables.description: If the patient has congestive heart failure

variables.name: Caucasian
variables.description: Caucasian parental proportion

variables.name: DVT
variables.description: If warfarin was indicated to manage deep vein thrombosis

variables.name: Diabetes
variables.description: If the patient has diabetes (either type I or II)

variables.name: Height
variables.description: Height measured at enrollment

variables.name: Native
variables.description: Native (Zapotecas) parental proportion

variables.name: Other
variables.description: If the patient has other conditions such as: hypertension, hypercholesterolemia, asthma, benign prostate hyperplasia, depression, glaucoma, osteoporosis, etc.

variables.name: PE
variables.description: If warfarin was indicated to manage pulmonary embolism

variables.name: Sex
variables.description: Gender of participant

variables.name: Smoking
variables.description: If the patient smokes

variables.name: Statins
variables.description: If the patient uses statins

variables.name: Stroke
variables.description: If warfarin was indicated to manage stroke

variables.name: Subject_ID
variables.description: De-identified Subject ID

variables.name: Valve
variables.description: If warfarin was indicated to manage mechanical heart valve replacement

variables.name: Warf_Daily_Dose
variables.description: Maintenance warfarin daily dose (under stable INR)

variables.name: Warf_Resistant
variables.description: If the patient is resistant to warfarin

variables.name: Warf_Sensitive
variables.description: If the patient is sensitive to warfarin

variables.name: Warf_Week_Dose
variables.description: Maintenance warfarin weekly dose (under stable INR)

variables.name: Weight
variables.description: Weight at the moment of giving informed consent

tables.description: This sample attributes table contains subject IDs, sample IDs, body site, analyte type, tumor status, histological type, analysis type, genotyping and sequencing technologies, and name of the centers which conducted genotyping or sequencing.

variables.name: Analyte_Type
variables.description: Analyte Type

variables.name: Body_Site
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: Genotyping_Center
variables.description: Name of the center which conducted genotyping

variables.name: Genotyping_Technology
variables.description: Genotyping technology used to analyze the samples

variables.name: Histological_Type
variables.description: Cell or tissue type or subtype of sample

variables.name: Is_Tumor
variables.description: Tumor status

variables.name: Sample_ID
variables.description: De-identified Sample ID

variables.name: Sequencing_Center
variables.description: Name of the center which conducted sequencing

variables.name: Sequencing_Technology
variables.description: Sequencing technology used to analyze the samples

variables.name: Subject_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: Type_Analysis
variables.description: Genotyping or Sequencing

tables.description: The subject consent file includes subject IDs, consent information, subject aliases, and case control status of the subject for Warfarin Sensitivity.

variables.name: Affection_Status
variables.description: Case control status of the subject for Warfarin Sensitivity. Controls are subjects with warfarin requirements of 4-6mg/day. Cases are subjects with warfarin stable dose <4mg/day or >6mg/day

variables.name: Consent
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

variables.name: Source_Subject_ID
variables.description: Subject ID used in the Source Repository

variables.name: Subject_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: Subject_Source
variables.description: Source repository where subjects originate

tables.description: This data table contains a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs. The data table also includes sample aliases and sample use.

variables.name: SAMPLE_USE
variables.description: Sample Use

variables.name: Sample_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: Sample_Source
variables.description: Source repository where samples originate

variables.name: Source_Sample_ID
variables.description: Sample ID used in the Source Repository

variables.name: Subject_ID
variables.description: Subject ID
